Plain-language tuning feedback for younger students — tells them which direction to tune in simple terms.

PITCH STABILITY · LAST 5s
RATE—Hz · DEPTH—¢
The graph plots pitch accuracy over the last 5 seconds — a flat line means steady intonation. STROBE is a professional-grade meter: the pattern locks completely still when you're perfectly in tune. VIBRATO measures the speed (Hz) and width (cents) of pitch oscillation on sustained notes.
TUNER — chromatic pitch detection via microphone. Play any note to see its name, octave, and how sharp or flat it is. Tap the mic button to start.
String Guide
CAPO 0
LISTENING…
Strings are shown low to high from the bottom up — same as tab notation. While the mic is on, the closest string highlights in cyan with your cents deviation and turns green when in tune. Tap any string row to hear it played back.
TOOLS — ten utilities in four folders: Pitch & Sound (piano, tonal center), Harmony (chords, circle of fifths, guitar charts), Rhythm (drum sequencer), and Analysis & Reference (volume meter, transposer, vocal range).

DANGERloud — risk of hearing damage

Readings are relative (dBFS). Actual SPL depends on your mic and device.
NIOSH: 85 dB SPL = 8 hr limit · 100 dB = 15 min · 110 dB = 2 min.

Circle of Fifths
Tap any key to explore
Outer ring = major keys. Inner ring = relative minors. Adjacent keys share 6 of 7 notes — the closer they are, the more related.
🎙
Vocal Range
A guided assessment that maps your tessitura, comfortable range, and physiological extremes — and tracks them over time.
Before you begin
🔇
Quiet room. Background noise confuses pitch detection.
💧
Hydrated, rested voice. Don't test after a long session or when unwell.
🚫
No forcing. Map your real voice — stop if anything feels strained.
🎤
Consistent distance. Hold your device ~20–30 cm from your mouth throughout.
About 4–5 minutes. The optional push steps can be skipped for a quick result.
Before We Start
Voice Category
Knowing whether you're classifying a male or female voice narrows the match pool and gives you a much more accurate result.
Step 1 of 6 — Warmup
Warm Up Your Voice
Cold vocal cords produce unreliable results and are more prone to strain. This three-part warmup takes about 90 seconds and will meaningfully improve your assessment accuracy.
1
Gentle Hum · 20s
Closed-mouth hum on a comfortable mid-range note. Let the vibration sit in your face, not your throat. This gets blood flowing to the cords without any strain.
2
Lip Trills · 30s
Blow air through loosely closed lips to make a "brrrr" sound, then slide gently up and down your range. One of the most effective cord warmups — low effort, high impact.
3
Sirens · 20s
Slide slowly from your lowest comfortable note up to your highest, then back down — like a siren. Keeps it easy and connected, no pushing. This stretches the full working range gently.
Stop any exercise if something feels uncomfortable. A brief warmup is better than none at all.
Exercise 1 of 3 — Gentle Hum
Closed-mouth hum on a comfortable note. Let the vibration sit in your face.
BREATHE
IN
Inhale
Breathe in deeply through your nose…

DRUMKIT step sequencer
BPM 90
TIME
GRID
BARS
SWING 0% ↑
tap = hit  ·  long-press = ghost
tap = hit · long-press = ghost/flam · hold label = precision
swing needs off-beat hits to be audible — try ghost notes on odd steps
Each row is a drum track. Tap a step to add a hit — it lights up. Long-press for a ghost note (quieter, shown dimmer) or flam (double strike). Hold the track label on the left to open the precision editor for that row. Load a PRESET below to hear a complete pattern, then modify it from there.

METRO — metronome with groove patterns and live tempo analysis. Set a BPM, tap to set tempo by feel, or switch to GROOVE mode for a 16-step accent pattern. ANALYSE tab uses the mic to check if you're rushing or dragging.

Use TAP to set tempo by feel — tap the button along with a song at least 4 times and it will calculate the BPM for you. The Italian name beneath the number (Andante, Allegro, etc.) is the traditional tempo marking for that speed.

Each square is one 16th-note step. Tap once for a soft hit, tap again for an accent, tap a third time to clear it. Beat 1 starts at the left — the vertical lines mark each quarter-note beat.

Your Tempo · Live Analysis

You
120
Target
PLAY OR TAP RHYTHMICALLY · MIC ON
Turn on the mic and play rhythmically. The app detects your tempo and tells you if you're rushing, dragging, or locking in.
120
1
TAP ANYWHERE TO CLOSE
TRAIN — practice exercises that earn XP. Ear Training: interval recognition, chord identification, sight singing (all use the mic). Rhythm: tempo lock, tempo guess, polyrhythm (tap-based, no mic needed). Scales: hear or improvise over any scale.
PRACTICE LAB
ear training · drills · exercises
Exercises
Explore
Coming Soon
RHYTHM
tempo · timing · cross-pulse
Tempo Lock: the click plays for a few bars then drops out — keep tapping on the beat until it returns. Your grade reflects how well you held the tempo. Tempo Guess: hear an unknown BPM and name it. Polyrhythm: feel two different pulse rates simultaneously — start with 3:2 (three against two).
EAR TRAINING
pitch · intervals · chords · singing
Interval Training: hear two notes and identify or sing the gap between them — the building block of all ear training. Chord Ear Training: hear a chord and name its quality (major, minor, dominant 7th, etc.). Sight Singing: the app plays a note and you sing it back — the mic verifies your pitch in real time. Turn on the mic before starting any of these.
INTERVAL TRAINING
match the target interval by ear

Rounds
RANGE
DIR
PLAYS
AUTO
TONE
VOL
100%
RANGE: how wide the intervals are (within one octave, or spanning beyond). DIR: whether the interval plays ascending, descending, or both. PLAYS: harmonic = both notes at once; melodic = one after the other. Use EDIT INTERVALS (top-left) to control which intervals appear in your rounds — start with Easy and expand gradually.

N:M means N pulses on top, M pulses on the bottom. 3:2 is the gateway. Higher ratios get progressively harder.

YOUR ANSWER
SPEED
PLAY
AUTO
TONE
VOL
100%
SPEED: how fast the notes of the arpeggio play — slower gives more time to hear each interval inside the chord. Use EDIT CHORDS (top-left) to control which chords appear; start with Easy (the four basic triads) before adding 7ths and extensions. Root Hidden is significantly harder — only try it once the Root Shown version feels easy.

SETTINGS
app preferences
Language
Note Naming
Teaching Mode
Help Tips
Hides the small ? buttons throughout the app. Tours still available via the header button.
Haptic Feedback
Vibrate on beats & correct answers. Metronome silent mode is in the METRO tab.
Latency Measurement
Tap the button each time you hear the click. The ball hits the line exactly when audio is scheduled — the gap between visual and what you hear is your device's audio delay.
—
Tap Calibration
38 ms offset
If taps in tempo exercises score "late," drag this up. Bluetooth audio usually needs 150-300ms; built-in speakers 30-80ms.
